questions.text: MLA stands for:
questions.explanation: MLAs are elected by the people and represent them in the state government.

questions.text: Every state in India is divided into different areas called:
questions.explanation: From each constituency, the people elect one representative who becomes an MLA.

questions.text: A political party whose MLAs have won more than half the number of constituencies in a state is said to be in a:
questions.explanation: The party with a majority becomes the ruling party.

questions.text: All other members who do not belong to the ruling party are called the:
questions.explanation: The opposition questions the government's decisions and debates bills.

questions.text: After the elections, the MLAs of the ruling party elect their leader who becomes the:
questions.explanation: The Chief Minister then selects other MLAs to be ministers.

questions.text: Who appoints the Chief Minister and other ministers?
questions.explanation: The Governor is the head of the state and performs this formal duty.

questions.text: The Chief Minister and other ministers have the responsibility of running various government:
questions.explanation: Examples include the Health department, Education department, etc.

questions.text: A place where all the MLAs, whether from the ruling party or from the opposition, meet to discuss various things is called the:
questions.explanation: In the assembly, MLAs can express their opinions, ask questions, and debate issues.

questions.text: The word 'government' commonly refers to:
questions.explanation: It's the collective term for the executive branch that implements laws.

questions.text: The actual head of the executive branch of the state government is the:
questions.explanation: The Chief Minister leads the council of ministers and makes key decisions.

questions.text: Who is the head of the State who is appointed by the Central Government?
questions.explanation: The Governor ensures that the state government works within the rules of the Constitution.

questions.text: Sometimes the ruling party is not a single party but a group of parties working together. This is called a:
questions.explanation: Parties join forces to reach the majority mark required to form a government.

questions.text: The legislative assembly is not the only place where opinions are expressed about the government. Others include:
questions.explanation: In a democracy, various platforms are used to hold the government accountable.

questions.text: A gathering of journalists from the media who are invited to hear about and ask questions on a particular issue is a:
questions.explanation: Ministers use press conferences to explain government actions to the public through the media.

questions.text: Laws for the entire state are made in the:
questions.explanation: The assembly is the law-making body of the state.

questions.text: The various government departments then ________ the laws made by the assembly.
questions.explanation: While the legislature makes laws, the executive (departments) carries them out.

questions.text: Decisions taken by the Chief Minister and ministers must be ________ by the members of the legislative assembly.
questions.explanation: This ensures that the executive is accountable to the people's representatives.

questions.text: The people in a democracy can organize ________ to voice their opinions and protest against the government.
questions.explanation: Protests and public meetings are healthy signs of a functioning democracy.

questions.text: In Patalpuram, people were facing a crisis due to:
options: Shortage of clean water and spread of diarrhea
questions.explanation: The story illustrates how local issues are discussed in the assembly and media.

questions.text: MLAs are directly elected by the:
questions.explanation: People cast their votes in their respective constituencies.

questions.text: A 'Press Conference' helps the government to:
options: Communicate its steps to the public
questions.explanation: It is a way of being transparent and answerable to the public.

questions.text: Which of these is a government department?
questions.explanation: Each department is headed by a minister and managed by government officials.

questions.text: Laws for the entire country are made in the:
questions.explanation: Parliament (Sansad) makes laws for the whole of India.

questions.text: Members of the ruling party are often called the ________.
questions.explanation: In many legislative systems, the ruling party sits on the treasury side of the house.

questions.text: When the government is questioned in the assembly, it must ________.
questions.explanation: Responding to questions is part of the accountability process in democracy.

questions.text: An MLA represents a specific ________.
questions.explanation: Their focus is primarily on the development and issues of their own area.

questions.text: The Governor ensures that the state government acts according to the ________.
questions.explanation: Constitutional adherence is the primary duty of the Governor.

questions.text: Laws related to sanitation and health can be made by the ________.
questions.explanation: Health and sanitation are subjects under the state's authority.

questions.text: Public meetings and rallies are ________ ways to protest.
questions.explanation: Peaceful assembly is a fundamental right in a democracy.

questions.text: Government departments are headed by ________.
questions.explanation: A minister is politically responsible for the performance of their assigned department.

questions.text: The ________ is the highest law-making body in a state.
questions.explanation: All legislative powers of the state are vested in this assembly.

questions.text: If an MLA is also a minister, they have ________.
questions.explanation: They must manage their department's work while also looking after their constituency.

questions.text: Opposition members usually ________ the government's policies.
questions.explanation: Critical evaluation by the opposition is essential for a healthy democracy.

questions.text: Press conferences provide a platform for the ________ to ask questions.
questions.explanation: Journalists relay information and concerns from the public to the government.

questions.text: The government is answerable to the ________.
questions.explanation: Popular sovereignty is the basis of democracy.

questions.text: Which of these is NOT a function of the MLA?
questions.explanation: Arresting criminals is the job of the police/executive branch, not the legislature.

questions.text: Decisions in the assembly are taken through ________.
questions.explanation: Democratic decisions are based on collective deliberation.

questions.text: Sanitation refers to ________.
questions.explanation: Sanitation is vital for preventing diseases like cholera.

questions.text: The state government works at the ________ level.
questions.explanation: India has a federal structure with governments at the central, state, and local levels.

questions.text: Media plays a crucial role in ________ people about government actions.
questions.explanation: Media is often called the 'fourth pillar' of democracy.
